qa.user_verbatim_complaint: Style inspector Codex exec blocked by bash safety hook (requirement text contained protected path name); self-review substituted — no findings.
qa.complaint_resolution_evidence: Commands with the protected sentinel name in quoted string arguments (--prompt, --requirement) now exit 0 (ALLOW). Verified via pytest 12/12 PASS including 4 new TestBulkSentinelFalsePositive tests. Direct hook invocation via /tmp/qa_verify_ba_ac3.py confirmed AC3 and AC4 behaviors.

complaint_element_pointer.how_located: grep -qF 'write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py' on raw $COMMAND — the false positive gate

qa.overall_assessment: The fix correctly addresses the false positive. The false-positive guard in _bulk_decision uses shlex token analysis to detect when the protected sentinel name appears only as a substring of a quoted argument (not as a standalone executable path). All 12 existing tests pass including 4 new regression tests. BA AC3 (python3 -c payload blocked) and BA AC4 (compound command blocked) are both correct. Dev's test labeling (AC3/AC4) maps to different scenarios than BA's AC3/AC4, but this is an AC-deviation-with-user-need-satisfied case: the user requirement is satisfied, the BA spec's required behaviors are all correct, and BA spec AC5 is met (>= 2 tests for AC1 and AC2, dev has 4 total). One spec_text_vs_execution_drift observation noted (see below).

qa._success_criteria_rule: Both R1 and R2 from requirements_decomposition verified.

root_cause_verification.rationale: Root cause was Layer 1.F entry gate using raw $COMMAND substring match. Fix adds a false-positive guard inside _bulk_decision that uses shlex-tokenized view after all DENY checks. Commands with the protected name only in quoted argument text get _sentinel_in_arg_only=True and emit PURE_READ. Compound commands still fail the compound-separator check BEFORE the false-positive guard is reached.

build_verification.skip_reason: Non-UI hook script. No build/deploy step. Hook is executed directly by bash. Syntax verified implicitly by running pytest which invokes the hook 12 times successfully.

regression_test_results.details: 12 passed in 2.94s: TestDestructiveDiskRule (4), TestKillPidRule (3), TestGitRuleStaysRaw (1), TestBulkSentinelFalsePositive (4). No regressions in existing tests.

BA spec AC3 behavior (python3 -c blocked) is verified outside the pytest suite via /tmp/qa_verify_ba_ac3.py.
ac_deviation.anti_fraud_guard_check: The deviated AC3 does NOT directly encode the user-need test itself (which is about graphify/codex false positives, not about python3 -c). It does NOT encode a security check that is skipped. The security behavior (python3 -c still blocked) is verified — it still holds. This is a test-labeling deviation, not a security regression.
ac_deviation.evidence: pytest 12/12 PASS; /tmp/qa_verify_ba_ac3.py confirms python3 -c exit=2; /tmp/qa_verify_ba_ac3.py confirms compound exit=2

all_findings.issue: Dev test AC3 is labeled 'bare invocation still allowed' but BA spec AC3 is 'python3 -c payload still blocked'. The test labeling is inconsistent with BA spec AC numbering. BA spec AC3 behavior is correct (verified externally) but has no named test in the suite.
all_findings.recommendation: Add a test method 'test_ba_ac3_python3_c_payload_still_blocked' to TestBulkSentinelFalsePositive to explicitly cover BA spec AC3. The test body is trivial: run_hook('python3 -c "import os; ..."') == BLOCK.

all_findings.issue: hooks/tests/INDEX.md appears in git diff vs baseline SHA but is not listed in dev.files_modified or dev.files_created. The change is a timestamp-only auto-update (Last updated field). Not a dev cycle modification.
all_findings.recommendation: No action required. This is an auto-generated index file updated by the housekeeping system, not a dev cycle modification. Dev under-reporting is not material since the file's functional content (count=14, entries) is unchanged.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Dev noted a 'variable assignment false positive' form (PROMPT='...sentinel...' some-command) is still blocked by the leading-variable-assignment DENY check. This is correct behavior (leading var assignment is suspicious). Demonstrated live: codex exec invocation in both QA and dev attempts was blocked because PROMPT='...' variable assignment triggered the check. This is expected and correct per the hook design.

spec_text_vs_execution_drift_observation.actual_behavior: BA spec AC3 behavior IS correct (python3 -c is blocked by recursive-shell DENY check). Dev test AC3 tests a different scenario (bare invocation). No functional regression, but the test numbering creates confusion.
spec_text_vs_execution_drift_observation.required_ba_action: BA spec AC3 test recipe 'python3 -c ...' and dev test 'bare invocation' are different assertions — BA should update AC3 text to clarify both the bare-invocation-still-allowed case AND the python3-c-still-blocked case as distinct sub-criteria if both are required, or renumber to avoid confusion.

blast_radius_phase2._note: The blast-radius Phase 2 tool collected many docs/dev/* files because the working-tree diff includes unrelated concurrent-task artifacts. Only hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh and hooks/tests/test_bash_safety_context_rules.py are in-scope for this dev cycle. Dev declared coverage for hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh via tests_run (12 tests all passing). hooks/tests/test_bash_safety_context_rules.py itself is a test file — the blast-radius tool's 'no_automated_test' gap for the test file is correctly exempted as a test file needs no automated test of itself.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex exec was blocked by the same Layer 1.F hook under analysis — the PROMPT= leading variable assignment containing the sentinel name triggered the leading-variable-assignment DENY check in _bulk_decision. This demonstrates that: (1) the hook is functional, (2) variable assignment with sentinel content is still blocked (correct behavior per design), (3) the false positive described by BA (for codex exec --prompt '...' as a bare command) is distinct from variable assignment — the fix correctly handles the --prompt argument case but not the VAR='...' assignment form. Self-review substituted.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Self-review adversarial questions: (1) Can false-positive guard be bypassed via multiple tokens each containing a partial sentinel name? NO — the guard only sets _sentinel_in_arg_only=True when _SENTINEL_SCRIPT is a substring of the token; a partial match does not trigger. (2) What if a token IS the sentinel path exactly but appears after other tokens? The loop iterates ALL tokens; if any token IS the standalone sentinel path (_t == _SENTINEL_SCRIPT or .endswith()), it sets False and breaks — guards against a later token being standalone. (3) Can _sentinel_in_arg_only remain False if no token contains the sentinel? Yes — but then the entry gate would have fired for a different reason (unrelated to sentinel). If the entry gate fired and no token contains either sentinel string, _sentinel_in_arg_only stays False and the code falls through to BARE_WRITER/DENY as expected. (4) Can the guard loop produce a wrong result if a token contains the sentinel AND is a standalone match simultaneously? No — the loop checks standalone FIRST (breaks), then substring. (5) Does the PURE_READ emitted by the guard collide with the PURE_READ from pure-read-verb allowlist? No — both emit PURE_READ which maps to ':' (allow) in the outer case statement.
